beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home while checking the rain forecast
beats.reasoning: Tuesday morning after a low-energy yesterday (45/100); grey drizzle means Nia stays cozy indoors longer. No rush given the mood and weather.

beats.activity: Commute to First Hill library branch via bus and light rail
beats.reasoning: Rain and SR-520 closure eastbound don't affect south-to-downtown commute; light rail is reliable in drizzle. Standard 35-min commute window.

beats.activity: Morning shift at First Hill library: reference desk, shelving, planning for teen book club
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library - First Hill Branch (First Hill)
beats.reasoning: Steady rain until 2pm makes indoor library work ideal; no pressure to leave early today. Focused work morning before lunch break.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby café, stay dry indoors
beats.reasoning: Rain still falling at noon; Nia grabs quick meal within walking distance of library rather than venturing far. Sarcastic Seattle move: embrace the weather indoors.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift at library: patron assistance, cataloging, reference desk
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library - First Hill Branch (First Hill)
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Nia stays through end of shift. Low energy yesterday means routine work day, no side errands.

beats.activity: Commute home via light rail and bus
beats.reasoning: Weather clearing; standard 40-min commute home after full shift. No detours needed; SR-520 eastbound closure doesn't affect south Seattle routes.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and eat at home: simple pasta and salad
beats.reasoning: Nia's pattern from yesterday (making pasta at home); low energy continues. Quiet evening at home suits the mood and drizzly day.

beats.activity: Video gaming: deep dive into a cozy game to decompress after work; Review and edit photos from past hikes on film camera, wind down
beats.reasoning: Nia loves video games and low energy post-work. Drizzly evening = perfect vibe for indoor gaming without guilt. Values comfort over crowds.
Full 8.25-hour sleep after a steady, low-key day. Ends at 6am the next calendar day, spanning overnight as required. No commute directly before sleep.

diary: Woke to that familiar Seattle patter on the roof—rain all morning, the kind that makes you grateful for indoor work. The commute was surprisingly smooth despite the SR-520 mess; I guess everyone else was stuck on the eastside. The First Hill branch felt cozy today, all grey light filtering through the windows. Helped a regular find obscure poetry collections, which never gets old. Grabbed lunch at Voila and just sat there with my coffee, watching the rain streak the windows. Afternoon flew by—cataloging new acquisitions, planning next month's teen book club (we're doing graphic novels). By the time I got home, I was ready to disappear. Fired up that cozy farming sim I've been playing, let my brain just... settle. Later I sorted through some old film photos from a hike last month—nothing fancy, just trees and light. It's the kind of day that feels complete without being eventful. Peaceful, really.
thought_process: The rain shaped everything today—it made the world feel smaller, more contained. I wasn't rushing; the weather gave me permission to just be present at each moment. The bridge closure didn't affect me directly since I take the light rail, but it made me aware of how fragile our infrastructure is, how easily systems can strain. After hours of patron interaction and problem-solving at the desk, I needed to decompress, so the gaming made sense—something with no stakes, just gentle progress. Reviewing those hiking photos felt like a natural wind-down, a way to reflect without effort. My mood stayed steady all day, neither up nor down, just grounded. Sometimes those grey Tuesday afternoons are exactly what you need.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
